The witching hour had hit at GroupLens. It was 3 PM and, like clockwork, Nick and Dexter had arrived at Jason's place. What would the conversation for today be? One thing you do know is that when 3 PM hits the lab the productivity hits the fan. Jason was nervous today though. He had been waiting to start his journey for the past 3 months and finally the day had come. It was the final day of classes before the December Holiday break. All his finals were done and assignments were in. Jason had only one more semester of college left. He knew that if he was ever going to graduate then he had to leave now in order to be back in time for the Spring 2026 semester. Little did he know what lay ahead of him in a trip that would change his life in ways he never imagined. [SEP] Nick had never climbed a mountain. He had conquered a few mountains, figuratively, in his life, but never had he seen the world from over 14,000 feet. While he had been on top numerous times in his life (if you know what I mean), this would be the ultimate experience. As Nick prepared to leave, a tear came to his eye, knowing that he might never make it back alive to the city he loved. [SEP] More tears came to his eyes when he remembered he had invited his pesky friend Dexter to come along on his journey. Jason, being driven by the goal at hand, started envisioning their destination: the snowcapped peaks of Mt. Hood. It was no Everest, but when you have never been mountain climbing before, Oregon's highest peak was exotic enough. Jason started to take mental inventory for the journey: parkas, sherpas, St. Bernards with multitudes of firewater... these gentlemen were as ready as they ever could be. [SEP] But really, Jason's biggest fear wasn't the climb, the cold, the crevasses that had consumed so many climbers in recent years... it was the journey-long separation from his first love, his true love: technology. With only a GPS and some crappy communication gadgets to appease his needs, this was going to be a very trying journey. [SEP] At this point in his musings, Jason became distracted by a leathery scraping sound. It slid through the car rhythmically, like the sound of a young maiden shucking corn. Doll-sized bundles of filth and fury, the bilious mal-creatures writhed into acclimation with the bright light of the car. Jason observed this unholy birth with shock-glazed calm.

Reese was in it to win it. Win what? Didn't matter. As long as there were people around to beat, she was determined to come out on top. In this case, the setting was high school. History class. Senior year. [SEP] Matthew was a young teacher but had been around enough to know Reese's type. Yeah, she knew her stuff but damn was she annoying. It gave her a bit of an attitude, knowing she could do no wrong. [SEP] Years later, as an accomplished attorney, she decided to run for office. Her opposition decided to do some research on her to see if there was any dirt they could dig up. Naturally, they looked through her yearbook. [SEP] They found nothing until the very last page. But surrounding her in the photo are four people with plaid shirts. They look normal enough, but it would later come out that they were running an extortion ring at the high school. Reese was the bait. They'd frame teachers and keep quiet in exchange for better grades, good recommendations, and occasionally money. Reese had managed to keep her name clear, but this photo was enough for the opposing campaign to use against her. Guilt by association.
